# UC-22.2.16 · NIS2 Supply Chain Third-Party Risk Continuous Monitoring

## Description

Uses accelerated network-traffic summaries tagged for supplier destinations to highlight oversized daily uploads, overdue third-party risk reviews, or an unusually wide set of internal clients talking to one supplier API.

## Value

Risk committees see spikes in data movement toward a vendor before a news-driven audit question lands, and they get a calendar view of which supplier reassessments slipped past 12 months without paperwork.

## Implementation

(1) In ES (263), assign `category=supplier` to third-party CIDRs/domains under **Configure → Data Enrichment → Asset Management**. (2) Build `supplier_risk_lookup` CSV with dest (IP or FQDN), supplier_name, risk_tier, last_assessment_date. (3) Enable **Network_Traffic** datamodel acceleration for **30 days** minimum. (4) Validate proxy field mapping to `All_Traffic.bytes_out`. (5) Add AWS CloudTrail (1876) panels if suppliers use cross-account roles.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) with licensed Assets & Identities; analyst training on `dest_category` tagging workflows under **ES menu → Configure**.
• Indexes feeding the CIM must include **Network_Traffic** tags — commonly `index=proxy` plus any **internal span** forwarders sending NetFlow via **Splunk Stream** (optional app 1809).
• Search head cluster needs **8 GB** Java heap minimum per heavy user concurrency when running spanning `tstats` across 24h slices.
• Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876) if CloudTrail supplements on-prem proxy blind spots.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize proxy logs: confirm `dest`, `src`, `bytes_out`, `app` fields exist. Use **props.conf** on indexers to rename vendor keys.
Import supplier inventory into ES assets with `category=supplier` and **priority** set so `All_Traffic.dest_category` resolves. Without ES, emulate with automatic lookup at search time (higher cost).
Schedule nightly CSV refresh for `supplier_risk_lookup` from your GRC API extract saved to `lookups/` under change control.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the provided `tstats` with `span=1d` for last **7 days** first; watch job inspector for scanned buckets. Save as `UC-22.2.16_supplier_risk_daily`.
Set alert when `bytes_out > 1073741824` **AND** `risk_tier=critical` for paging; use lower threshold for informational dashboards.
Optional complement: clone search replacing `summariesonly=false` if acceleration warming incomplete — expect longer runtime.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick supplier IP known from contract; run raw `index=proxy dest=x.x.x.x` verifying byte sums roughly equal `tstats` output for same day.
Check `strptime` on `last_assessment_date` handles timezone: use `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S` if ISO timestamps were exported.
Confirm `internal_sources` interpretable — large SaaS front ends may inflate unique `src` due to NAT; cross-check with firewall logs.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard layout: Row1 SLA tiles (overdue assessments, TB to suppliers), Row2 timeline `timechart avg(bytes_out) by supplier_name`, Row3 detail table.
Route critical alerts to `#third-party-risk` Slack via webhook; include deep link to Splunk drilldown prefilled with **earliest=-24h**.
Monthly compliance pack: export PDF plus raw CSV stored into `index=audit_evidence` sourcetype=`nis2_supply_chain`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **tstats empty** — Network_Traffic model not accelerated within time window; visit **Settings → Data models**.
• **dest_category always null** — asset merge failing; verify **asset key** format (CIDR vs IP).
• **bytes_out zero** — vendor logs measure only sessions; map to `sent_bytes` via `eval` in props.
• **Lookup missing rows** — dest formatted as FQDN but lookup lists IP — maintain dual columns `dest_ip` and `dest_fqdn`.
• **False overdue** — clock skew on CSV export; regenerate using UTC normalized timestamps.

## SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out dc(All_Traffic.src) as internal_sources
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  where All_Traffic.dest_category="supplier"
  by All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.app span=1d
| rename All_Traffic.* as *
| lookup supplier_risk_lookup dest OUTPUT supplier_name, risk_tier, last_assessment_date
| eval days_since_assessment=round((now()-strptime(last_assessment_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400, 0)
| eval assessment_overdue=if(days_since_assessment > 365, 1, 0)
| where bytes_out > 1073741824 OR assessment_overdue=1 OR internal_sources > 50
| sort - bytes_out
| table dest, supplier_name, risk_tier, bytes_out, internal_sources, days_since_assessment, assessment_overdue
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table of supplier rows with bytes_out and assessment flags, bar chart of top destinations, single value count of overdue assessments.
